title: curcas portoricensis
intro: Curcas portoricensis, also known as the Portorican Strangler Fig or the Strangler Fig, is a species of plant in the fig family native to the island of Puerto Rico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including the potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of Curcas portoricensis typically involve using the leaves and bark to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
